programmes.show_name: Romesh Ranganathan: For the Love of Hip Hop
programmes.show_description: 50 Cent joins Romesh to talk about the legacy of Get Rich Or Die Tryin' on its 20th anniversary. Plus, tracks from Jurassic 5, A Tribe Called Quest and Jay-Z.
Tony is back with more fabulous Soul music celebrating 50 years of George Benson's Breezin', and birthdays for Chaka Khan, Stephanie Mills, Diana Ross and Aretha Franklin.
Vernon Kay presents highlights of the performances from this year's Piano Room Month, accompanied by the BBC Concert Orchestra in the BBC's Maida Vale studios.
Level 42 performing live at Hammersmith Odeon in 1985.  Featuring The Chinese Way, Leaving Me Now, The Chant Has Begun, Love Games, Turn It On, Micro Kid and How Can It Be.

programmes.show_name: The Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Angela Griffin's Pop Unwinds Mix with Wham, Abba, Take That and Spice Girls.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 60s with Tony Blackburn
programmes.show_description: Tony brings you two hours of music from the best decade ever, with hits from Engelbert Humperdinck, Helen Shapiro and The Kinks. This week, the top three comes from 1967.
Joel Dommett sits in and chats to Ed Gamble about his new upcoming tour Fresh Hell. Plus, The Mystery Voice and Bon Jovi vinyl.
Romesh brings the laughs, soundtracked by great music. As well as chatting to brilliant guests, he's sharing your hilarious stories, so you can start your Saturday with a smile!
Spend Saturday lunchtime with Emma and enjoy a menu full of your favourite music, great conversation and a delicious dive into the BBC archives for dessert.
Rylan is joined by Corinne Bailey Rae for a very special KaRYoke to celebrate 20 years since the release of her hit single Put Your Records On.
Shaun Keaveny with fabulous teatime tunes and your tales of the extraordinary and the everyday.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 80s with Gary Davies
programmes.show_description: Thomas Dolby shares his favourite 80s Moments, and there's a Pet Shop Boys 1986 Mastermix.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 90s with Fearne Cotton
programmes.show_description: Fearne's remembering classic 90s telly like This Life, Light Lunch with Mel and Sue, and The Racoons, with tunes from The Cardigans, Fugees and Todd Terry.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 90s with Fearne Cotton
programmes.show_description: This one's for the pop lovers! An hour of pop-tastic tunes from the likes of Boyzone, Kylie Minogue, Janet Jackson, Gina G, and Take That.

programmes.show_name: Michelle Visage: The Musicals That Changed My Life
programmes.show_description: Michelle Visage's love letter to the musicals that have changed her life.
...Changed My Life by Michael Ball. Michael Ball celebrates 40 years of Les Mis and reflects on how it changed his life.

programmes.show_name: Dance Sounds of the 90s with Vernon Kay
programmes.show_description: Vernon mixes up 90s dance tunes that packed out super club dance floors - perfect to wave your glow sticks to! Tunes from The Prodigy, JX, Sash!, Darude, ATB, Grace and many more!

programmes.show_name: The Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Angela Griffin brings you calm with The Mindful Melodies Unwinds Mix, featuring Coldplay, Kacey Musgraves and James Taylor.
Paul Young performing live in concert at Wolverhampton Civic Hall in 1997. Featuring Wherever I Lay My Hat (That's My Home), Love of the Common People and Every Time You Go Away.
Actress Holliday Grainger joins Vernon Kay to choose ten songs that have soundtracked her life. Holliday currently stars in series three of The Capture on BBC One & iPlayer.
The Reverend Kate Bottley and Jason Mohammad spread hope and joy, this morning they're joined by Faraaz Noor from The Traitors to reflect on Eid.
Your stories, dedications and classic love songs with Michael... easy like Sunday morning.
Paddy's live from Salford every Sunday, with The Weekest Link quiz and your Sunday Dinners.
Dame Elaine's got a run of New York show tunes and theatre news in her Broadway Bulletin! Plus, sunny songs to welcome ppring, and your Break A Leg messages!

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 70s with Shaun Keaveny
programmes.show_description: Shaun sits in for Bob and brings along with him a previously unheard interview with the late Neil Sedaka and 10cc's Graham Gouldman.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1983.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1994.
Tony Blackburn spins his personal pick of the hits from across the decades. To listen on most smart speakers just 'ask BBC Sounds to play Tony Blackburn's Golden Hour'.
Paul picks his favourite songs from the musicals  - which come from West Side Story, Willy Wonka, Sunset Boulevard, Funny Girl and Waitress. Plus, a tribute to Neil Sedaka.
Declutter the mind and feel calm with serene sounds from Diana Ross, Vanessa Williams and Finley Quaye.

programmes.show_name: Radio 2 Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Create your own corner of calm with The Spring Clean Unwinds Mix, featuring Taylor Swift, Tom Petty and Honeyz.
